text.tspan: Jellyfish are marine animals from the phylum
Cnidaria. They have drifted through Earth's oceans for
over 500 million years, using rhythmic bell contractions to move
through the water. Specialized stinging cells, called nematocysts,
help them capture prey and defend against predators.
Found in oceans worldwide, jellyfish are among the planet's oldest
surviving animal groups. Despite lacking several organs, they are highly
successful organisms that can sense light, gravity, and chemicals in their environment.
